# Testing Standards

> Generated by Velocity /init. Customize for your team.
> Referenced by: tdd skill, QA Agent, Engineer Agent context load.

## Philosophy

- TDD is the default implementation loop. Write the failing test first.
- Design interfaces for testability before writing implementation.
- Test behavior, not implementation details.
- One failing test at a time: red → green → refactor.

## Coverage Requirements

- Minimum test coverage: 80% (configured in `.velocity/guardrails/default.md`).
- Every slice must have tests at every layer it touches.
- New code without tests is not mergeable.

## Test Types

### Unit Tests

- Test one unit of behavior in isolation.
- No I/O, no network, no file system.
- Mock all external dependencies.
- Fast: under 100ms per test.

### Integration Tests

- Test interactions between components (e.g., service + database, service + message broker).
- Use real infrastructure (testcontainers, embedded DB, localstack) — not mocks for infrastructure.
- Test the critical path end-to-end within the service boundary.

### End-to-End Tests

- Test user-facing flows from UI to persistence and back.
- Cover the happy path and the most critical error paths per feature.
- Run in CI against a full deployment. Not on every commit.

## TDD Loop

```
1. Confirm behaviors to test for this slice
2. Design interface for testability (minimal, clear)
3. Write one failing test (red)
4. Write minimum implementation to pass (green)
5. Refactor: look for deep module opportunities
6. Repeat until slice is complete
```

## Test Naming

Tests must read as specifications:

- `should process payment when card is valid`
- `should reject payment when card is expired`
- `should emit PaymentProcessed event after successful charge`

## Stack-Specific Notes

> Velocity /init will add stack-specific testing guidance here based on detected test framework.
